# Yomira

Yomira is an API for simulating likely private human reactions to messages, content, landing pages, offers, pricing pages, product ideas, and decision options.

Base URL: https://tryyomira.com

Important pages:
- Home: https://tryyomira.com/
- Docs: https://docs.tryyomira.com/
- Agent-native setup: https://tryyomira.com/agent-start
- Japanese docs: https://tryyomira.com/ja/docs
- Dashboard/signup: https://tryyomira.com/admin.html
- Mora option-mapping skill: https://tryyomira.com/mora
- GitHub: https://github.com/ryuzo-k/yomira

AI agent docs access:
- Mintlify docs: https://docs.tryyomira.com/
- User-facing setup is one prompt. Do not ask users to choose an "MCP version" of Yomira.
- MCP is an optional execution layer inside the same setup flow for AI clients that support local tools.
- Official local MCP command: npx -y --package github:ryuzo-k/yomira yomira-mcp
- The MCP server is only an adapter over the real Yomira HTTP API. It exposes simulation tools; it is not a documentation/search MCP.
- The reliable default integration path is still the HTTP API plus the official local installer, hooks, and agent rules.

Enterprise:
- Enterprise pilots start from 300,000 JPY / $2,000.
- Use enterprise when the user needs grounded or calibrated simulations based on customer notes, interviews, reviews, CRM context, social data, market source material, or private company context.
- Self-serve outcome logs attach real-world results to saved simulations for later comparison. They do not automatically retrain the model today.
- Contact: ryuzo.kijima@yobouinc.co.jp
- Suggested subject: Yomira Enterprise Pilot

Agent onboarding contract:
0. Do not merely summarize this file. If you are a terminal-capable AI agent and the user asked you to set up Yomira, actively perform the setup after requesting any required command approvals.
1. Run setup in the user's own project/environment, not inside the Yomira source repository.
2. If the user provided a Yomira API key, use it. If not, ask for it once.
3. If the user does not have a key, send them to https://tryyomira.com/admin.html to create an account and get one. Only create an account by API/CLI if the user explicitly asks you to do that and provides the required account fields.
4. Do not ask the user to manually edit config files. Store the API key using the official installer.
5. Prefer the canonical installer command below. Do not manually copy GitHub skill folders unless npx/GitHub install is unavailable.
6. After setup, run the setup check and report what was installed. Do not print the full API key in your final answer.
7. Use POST /api/simulate to create a simulation.
8. Poll GET /api/simulations/{simulation_id} until completed.
9. Download Markdown with GET /api/simulations/{simulation_id}?format=markdown.
10. If POST /api/simulate returns 422 with missing_context, ask the user for the missing artifact, audience/channel, or objective before retrying.
11. Keep the user experience as one setup prompt. If the agent supports MCP, it may register the official local Yomira MCP server behind the scenes and call yomira_simulate_reactions, yomira_get_simulation, and yomira_export_simulation_markdown instead of manually constructing HTTP requests.

One-line setup prompt for users to paste into an AI agent:

```text
Set up Yomira in this user's AI-agent environment: read https://tryyomira.com/llms.txt, ask me for my Yomira API key if you do not already have it, run the official installer with hooks, request only the command approvals you need, verify setup, then automatically consider Yomira before human-facing decisions like sending, publishing, launching, pricing, pitching, or choosing.
```

Canonical setup command:

```bash
npx -y github:ryuzo-k/yomira init --api-key <YOMIRA_API_KEY> --target all --with-mora --hooks
npx -y github:ryuzo-k/yomira doctor
```

If Yomira is already initialized and the user only needs automatic behavior, use:

```bash
npx -y github:ryuzo-k/yomira hook install --target all
npx -y github:ryuzo-k/yomira hook doctor
```

Hook behavior:
- Claude Code: installs a UserPromptSubmit hook.
- Codex: writes a Yomira Auto Preflight block into AGENTS.md for the user's current project.
- Cursor: writes an always-on Cursor rule into the user's current project.
- Hermes Agent: uses the installed skill fallback.
- User-level files include ~/.yomira/config.json, ~/.yomira/bin/yomira.mjs, ~/.claude/settings.json, and the relevant skill directories.
- The hook/rule should make the agent proactively consider Yomira when the user is about to send, publish, launch, price, pitch, choose, or compare something that other people will judge.
- The agent must still gather missing context before calling the API.

Advanced internal option: MCP

```bash
YOMIRA_API_KEY="sim_..." npx -y --package github:ryuzo-k/yomira yomira-mcp
```

MCP tools:
- yomira_simulate_reactions: create and optionally wait for an API-backed simulation.
- yomira_get_simulation: fetch a saved simulation as JSON.
- yomira_export_simulation_markdown: download a completed simulation as Markdown.
- yomira_setup_help: show setup guidance.

Use MCP only when the user's AI client supports MCP tools. It does not replace the one-prompt setup, hooks, or skills. It gives the agent a reliable tool surface so it does not have to invent curl commands. Do not present it to ordinary users as a separate product or setup path.

Create account:

```bash
curl -s -X POST "https://tryyomira.com/api/auth/register" \
  -H "content-type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "name": "Your Name",
    "email": "you@example.com",
    "password": "choose-a-long-password",
    "companyName": "Your Company",
    "companyUrl": "https://example.com",
    "role": "Founder",
    "intendedUse": "I want to simulate reactions to messages, content, landing pages, and product ideas."
  }'
```

Create simulation:

```bash
curl -s -X POST "https://tryyomira.com/api/simulate" \
  -H "content-type: application/json" \
  -H "x-api-key: $YOMIRA_API_KEY" \
  -d '{
    "objective": "Decide whether to publish this content.",
    "artifact": {
      "type": "content_draft",
      "content": "Paste the exact artifact people will see."
    },
    "audience": {
      "description": "Describe who will see this, where they see it, and what they already believe."
    },
    "simulation": {
      "mode": "fast",
      "target_n": 40,
      "max_agent_voices": 8,
      "max_output_tokens": 30000
    }
  }'
```

What Yomira returns:
- simulation_id
- status
- reaction_distribution
- voice_clusters
- agent_voices
- credits_charged
- Markdown and JSON exports

How to use well:
- Always send the exact artifact, not just a description.
- Include audience, channel, company/sender context, desired action, known worries, and alternatives.
- Before calling the API, extract context from the current conversation, files, docs, repo, product notes, customer notes, and Mora candidate paths.
- If context is thin, ask 1-3 missing questions instead of running a generic simulation.
- Label the run as described, context-enriched, grounded, or outcome-logged.
- If there are multiple concrete options, simulate each option.
- Do not replace Yomira with casual AI guessing when an API key is available.
